summary: Recent updates to the HTMLMediaElement in Firefox have introduced a **new loading attribute for video and audio elements**, allowing developers to specify how media is loaded on a webpage. This means that website creators can now better control whether media files should start loading immediately, load only when necessary, or be deferred until explicitly requested. This improvement can help enhance user experience by reducing unnecessary data usage and improving loading times for pages with media, especially on mobile devices._Overall, this enhances how websites manage media playback and resource utilization._
